text:
Chapter 3: The Plant Cell

3.1 Cell Wall

The plant cell wall is composed of cellulose microfibrils. It provides structural support and protection.

The cell membrane lies just inside the cell wall. It is selectively permeable, controlling what enters and exits.

The cytoplasm contains various organelles including: - Chloroplasts (photosynthesis) - Mitochondria (cellular respiration) - Ribosomes (protein synthesis) - Endoplasmic reticulum (transport)

diagrams.label: Plant Cell
diagrams.description: A simplified cross-section diagram of a plant cell contained within a rectangular boundary. The cell structure is depicted by a large, outer boundary (representing the cell wall). Inside this boundary, the main internal component is a large, central circle labeled 'Nucleus'. The diagram illustrates the basic compartmentalization of a plant cell.

captions: Fig. 3.1: A typical plant cell showing major organelles.
